reasoning: The SOFR Index measures the cumulative impact of compounding the SOFR (Secured Overnight Financing Rate) each business day. Because SOFR is currently extremely positive (around 4.5%) and the Fed has never implemented negative policy rates, the index fundamentally must monotonically increase over time, as any positive interest rate compounded daily will strictly add to the index value. This makes it a near mathematical certainty that the index will be higher on any future date compared to May 25, 2025. Per the instructions, max probability is bounded and forecasts for distant dates must technically reflect slightly more uncertainty, hence the slight gradation towards 0.5, even though the fundamental likelihood of it being higher actually remains near absolute certainty across all horizons.
